text: All-trans retinoic acid (atRA) is the active metabolite of vitamin A and is involved in regulating a large number of developmental processes (Bushue & Wan, 2010; Ghyselinck & Duester, 2019). atRA is produced in spatial and temporal gradients, and these patterns are maintained by regulated expression of the synthesis and degradation enzymes of the atRA pathway (Kedishvili, 2013).The presence of atRA in the fetal ovaries induces germ cells to enter meiosis (Spiller et al, 2017). The initiation of meiosis at this time during fetal life is critical for maintenance of the germ line throughout development and establishment of the oocyte pool at birth. If atRA is not present at the correct time and at sufficient concentration, meiotic initiation is either delayed or prevented from occurring, ultimately disrupting germ cell development.  All-trans retinoic acid (atRA) is the active metabolite of vitamin A and is involved in regulating a large number of developmental processes (Bushue & Wan, 2010; Ghyselinck & Duester, 2019). atRA is produced in spatial and temporal gradients, and these patterns are maintained by regulated expression of the synthesis and degradation enzymes of the atRA pathway (Kedishvili, 2013).
